bill.short_title: Further Additional Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act, 2023

provisions.rate: at a rate for operations as provided in the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2023 (division A of Public Law 117-180)
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 101. The Continuing Appropriations Act, 2023 (division A of Public Law 117–180) is amended by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting ''December 30, 2022''.
notes: Extends the existing CR (P.L. 117-180, Division A) by amending the expiration date in section 106(3) to December 30, 2022.
This is a simple date extension of the prior CR; no new appropriations or anomalies are introduced in Division A.
Division A contains no individual account-level appropriations — it operates by extending the baseline CR mechanism already enacted.

cross_references.description: Strikes the prior expiration date and inserts December 30, 2022
Continuing Appropriations Act, 2023 — the CR being extended

provisions.statutory_reference: Section 309(j)(11) of the Communications Act of 1934 (47 U.S.C. 309(j)(11))
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: Section 309(j)(11) of the Communications Act of 1934 (47 U.S.C. 309(j)(11)) shall be applied by substituting ''December 30, 2022'' for ''December 23,
notes: Extends FCC spectrum auction authority by 7 days, from December 23, 2022 to December 30, 2022
Date substitution provision, no dollar amount involved

cross_references.target: Section 309(j)(11) of the Communications Act of 1934 (47 U.S.C. 309(j)(11))

provisions.statutory_reference: Section 235(b) of the Sentencing Reform Act of 1984 (18 U.S.C. 3551 note; Public Law 98–473; 98 Stat. 2032)
provisions.period: 35 years and 60 days from the original enactment of the Sentencing Reform Act
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text:
(b) 
Amendment of sentencing reform act of 1984
For purposes of section 235(b) of the Sentencing Reform Act of 1984 (18 U.S.C. 3551 note; Public Law 9
notes: May be cited as the United States Parole Commission Further Additional Extension Act of 2022
Changes references from '35 years and 46 days' to '35 years and 60 days', extending the Parole Commission by 14 additional days
Subsection (c) makes this effective as though enacted as part of the Further Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act, 2023
Subsection (d) supersedes Section 103 of division B of the Further Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act, 2023

cross_references.target: Section 235(b) of the Sentencing Reform Act of 1984 (Public Law 98-473)
cross_references.description: Extends Parole Commission authorization from 35 years and 46 days to 35 years and 60 days

cross_references.target: Further Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act, 2023
cross_references.description: Subsection (c) takes effect as though enacted as part of that Act; subsection (d) supersedes Section 103 of division B of that Act

provisions.program_name: Commodity Futures Trading Commission Customer Protection Fund Expenses Account
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: Section 1(b) of Public Law 117–25 (135 Stat. 297) shall be applied by substituting ''December 30, 2022'' for ''December 23, 2022'' each place it appea
notes: Extends CFTC Customer Protection Fund expenses account authorization by 7 days, from December 23, 2022 to December 30, 2022
Substitution applies 'each place it appears' in the referenced section

cross_references.description: Substitutes expiration date each place it appears

provisions.description: Extends a moratorium established under Section 424(a) of title IV of division G of Public Law 113-76 by substituting 'December 31, 2022' for 'December 24, 2022'.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: Section 424(a) of title IV of division G of Public Law 113–76 shall be applied by substituting ''December 31, 2022'' for ''December 24, 2022''.
notes: Extends an unspecified moratorium by 7 days, from December 24, 2022 to December 31, 2022
Public Law 113-76 is the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2014; Division G is the Financial Services and General Government Appropriations Act, 2014; Section 424 relates to a moratorium provision

cross_references.target: Section 424(a) of title IV of division G of Public Law 113-76

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from statutory PAYGO scorecards, Senate PAYGO scorecards, and budget scorekeeping under the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, and the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
notes: Standard PAYGO and budget scorekeeping exemption provision for the division
Subsection (a) exempts from Statutory PAYGO scorecards under section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Subsection (b) exempts from Senate PAYGO scorecards under section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)
Subsection (c) exempts from section 251 of BBEDCA, section 302(a) allocations under CBA, and paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of Statutory PAYGO

cross_references.target: Rule 3 of the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines (Conference Report 105-217)
Section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)
Section 250(c)(8) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

flagged_issues: Division A contains only a single section (SEC. 101) that extends the existing CR through December 30, 2022. No new dollar amounts or anomalies are specified in this division.
Division B contains no dollar-amount provisions; all are short-term date extensions (7-14 days) and budgetary exemptions
SEC. 103(d) supersedes Section 103 of division B of the Further Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act, 2023
SEC. 105 references a moratorium under P.L. 113-76 Division G Title IV Section 424(a) — the specific moratorium subject is not named in this bill text
